This process is essential for the fertilization of the ovules and the production of seeds. In angiosperms, pollination can occur through various methods, such as wind, water, or animal pollination. Animal pollinators, such as bees, butterflies, and hummingbirds, are important in the pollination of many angiosperms. Pollination does not occur in fungi. Fungi are a separate group of organisms that are different from plants and do not have flowers or produce seeds. Instead of reproducing through seeds, fungi reproduce through spores. These spores are produced in large numbers and are dispersed by wind or other means to grow into new fungi. In some cases, fungi can also reproduce asexually by producing new individuals from fragments of the parent fungus.

questions.explanation: The term pollination refers to the transfer of pollen from anther to stigma. Because pollens are found only in angiosperms and gymnosperms so this phenomenon relates to angiosperms and gymnosperms only.

questions.text: Female gametophyte of angiosperms is represented by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The ovule is a structure that is found in the female reproductive system of angiosperms, which are flowering plants. It is the structure that gives rise to the seed after fertilization. Each ovule contains an egg cell, which is fertilized by a sperm cell from the male gametophyte to form the zygote. The ovule also contains other cells, such as the megaspore mother cell, which undergoes meiosis to produce the megaspores that give rise to the female gametophyte. The ovule is surrounded by the integuments, which develop into the seed coat after fertilization.

The megaspore mother cell is a type of cell that is found in the ovule of angiosperms, which are flowering plants. It undergoes meiosis to produce four haploid megaspores, one of which develops into the female gametophyte. The female gametophyte contains the egg cell and other cells that are involved in fertilization. The megaspore mother cell is an important cell in the development of the ovule and the female reproductive system of angiosperms.

The embryo sac contains the female gametophyte, which is also haploid. The female gametophyte consists of seven cells, including the egg cell and two synergids, which are involved in the fertilization process. Hence, female gametophyte is represented by embryo sac.

The nucellus is the maternal tissue that surrounds the embryo sac in the ovule of a flowering plant. It is derived from the diploid integument, which is the outermost layer of the ovule. The nucellus is diploid.

questions.explanation: Female gametophyte of angiosperms is represented by embryo sac. The polygonum type of embryo sac contains 8­nuclei and 7­cells. It is found in more than 80% plant families. The nucleus of megaspore undergoes division and give rise to embryosac or female gametophyte by the process of megagametogenesis.

questions.text: Male gametophyte of angiosperms/monocots is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The microsporangium is a structure that is found in the male reproductive system of seed plants, including angiosperms, which are flowering plants. It is a structure that produces microspores, which develop into male gametophytes, or pollen grains. The microsporangium is usually located in the anther of the stamen and contains microsporocytes, which undergo meiosis to produce microspores. The microspores then develop into pollen grains, which contain the male gametophyte, or sperm cells. The microsporangium is an important structure in the reproductive system of seed plants and plays a crucial role in the development of pollen.

The nucellus is a part of the ovule in angiosperms, which are flowering plants. It is a central part of the ovule that surrounds the female gametophyte. The nucellus is responsible for providing nutrients to the developing female gametophyte and later to the developing embryo and endosperm after fertilization. The nucellus is also involved in the formation of the seed coat, which protects the embryo and endosperm after fertilization. The nucellus is an important part of the female reproductive system of angiosperms and plays a crucial role in the development of the seed.

In angiosperms, microspores are produced by the microsporangium, which is located in the anther of the stamen. The microsporangium contains microsporocytes, which undergo meiosis to produce microspores. The microspores then develop into pollen grains, which contain the male gametophyte, or sperm cells. In angiosperms, the pollen grain is typically composed of two cells: the generative cell, which will divide to produce two sperm cells, and the tube cell, which will grow into a pollen tube that delivers the sperm cells to the female reproductive structure of the plant. The pollen grain is carried from the anther to the stigma of the pistil by wind or pollinators, such as bees or butterflies.

The stamen is the male reproductive organ of angiosperms, which are flowering plants. It consists of two main parts: the anther and the filament. The anther contains the pollen sacs, which produce the pollen grains. The filament is a long, thin stalk that supports the anther. The stamen is responsible for producing and releasing the male gametophyte, or pollen, which contains the sperm cells that fertilize the egg cells in the female reproductive organs of the plant. The stamen is an important part of the reproductive system of angiosperms and plays a crucial role in the development of seeds and fruits.

questions.explanation: Male gametophyte of angiosperms is a microspore. Microspore is haploid, uninucleate, minute spores produced in large numbers as a result of meiosis in microspore mother cell inside the microsporangia. These are the first cell of gametophytic generations in angiosperms.
